devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । स इत्थं द्विजमुख्येन सह सङ्कथयन् हरिः । सर्वभूतमनोऽभिज्ञः स्मयमान उवाच तम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācasa itthaṃ dvijamukhyena saha saṅkathayan hariḥsarvabhūtamano'bhijñaḥ smayamāna uvāca tam
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
While Hari was conversing with the prominent Brāhmaṇa on such matters he, knowing as he did the minds of all beings, spoke to him smilingly.

devanagari: ब्रह्मण्यो ब्राह्मणं कृष्णो भगवान् प्रहसन् प्रियम् । प्रेम्णा निरीक्षणेनैव प्रेक्षन् खलु सतां गतिः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmaṇyo brāhmaṇaṃ kṛṣṇo bhagavān prahasan priyampremṇā nirīkṣaṇenaiva prekṣan khalu satāṃ gatiḥ
translation: Lord Kṛṣṇa was friendly to Brāhmaṇas and he was certainly the asylum of the pious. Looking at his dear Brāhmaṇa friend with an affectionate glance, he laughed loudly and asked in a joking mood:

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । किमुपायनमानीतं ब्रह्मन्मे भवता गृहात् । अण्वप्युपाहृतं भक्तैः प्रेम्णा भुर्येव मे भवेत् । भूर्यप्यभक्तोपहृतं न मे तोषाय...
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvācakimupāyanamānītaṃ brahmanme bhavatā gṛhātaṇvapyupāhṛtaṃ bhaktaiḥ premṇā bhuryeva me bhavetbhūryapyabhaktopahṛtaṃ na me toṣāya kalpate
translation:
The Lord said:
“What present have you brought for me from your house, O Brāhmaṇa? Even the slightest offering—a particle given with affection by my devotees is considered by me as very great, but even if a lot of presents are given to me by one who is not my votary, they are not conducive to my joy or satisfaction.

devanagari: पत्रं पुष्पं फलं तोयं यो मे भक्त्या प्रयच्छति । तदहं भक्त्युपहृतं अश्नामि प्रयतात्मनः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: patraṃ puṣpaṃ phalaṃ toyaṃ yo me bhaktyā prayacchatitadahaṃ bhaktyupahṛtaṃ aśnāmi prayatātmanaḥ
translation: A person who devoutly offers me a leaf, a flower, a fruit or even water, I enjoy that which has been brought to me with devotion by a person of controlled and pure mind.”

devanagari: इत्युक्तोऽपि द्विजस्तस्मै व्रीडितः पतये श्रियः । पृथुकप्रसृतिं राजन् न प्रायच्छदवाङ्मुखः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: ityukto'pi dvijastasmai vrīḍitaḥ pataye śriyaḥpṛthukaprasṛtiṃ rājan na prāyacchadavāṅmukhaḥ
translation: Even though the Brāhmaṇa was thus requested, he felt so much abashed that he did not hand over the present of pṛthukas (parched, flattened rice) to the Lord of the goddess Śrī and simply hung his head, O king.

devanagari: सर्वभूतात्मदृक् साक्षात् तस्यागमनकारणम् । विज्ञायाचिन्तयन्नायं श्रीकामो माभजत्पुरा ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvabhūtātmadṛk sākṣāt tasyāgamanakāraṇamvijñāyācintayannāyaṃ śrīkāmo mābhajatpurā
translation: But the Lord can directly see what goes in the minds of all beings and he knew definitely the cause of Śrīdāman’s visit. He thought to himself, “This Brāhmaṇa never approached me or worshipped me for money. This friend of mine has come here for pleasing his virtuous wife (in compliance of her entreaty). I shall bestow upon him wealth which is rare even for immortal gods.

devanagari: पत्‍न्याः पतिव्रतायास्तु सखा प्रियचिकीर्षया । प्राप्तो मामस्य दास्यामि सम्पदोऽमर्त्यदुर्लभाः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: pat‍nyāḥ pativratāyāstu sakhā priyacikīrṣayāprāpto māmasya dāsyāmi sampado'martyadurlabhāḥ
translation: But the Lord can directly see what goes in the minds of all beings and he knew definitely the cause of Śrīdāman’s visit. He thought to himself, “This Brāhmaṇa never approached me or worshipped me for money. This friend of mine has come here for pleasing his virtuous wife (in compliance of her entreaty). I shall bestow upon him wealth which is rare even for immortal gods.

devanagari: इत्थं विचिन्त्य वसनात् चीरबद्धान् द्विजन्मनः । स्वयं जहार किमिदं इति पृथुकतण्डुलान् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ vicintya vasanāt cīrabaddhān dvijanmanaḥsvayaṃ jahāra kimidaṃ iti pṛthukataṇḍulān
translation: Thinking thus within himself, he, of his own accord, snatched from under the clothes of the Brāhmaṇa, the parched flattened rice tied up in a rag (and brought by way of present to Śrī Kṛṣṇa) asking him, “What is it?

devanagari: नन्वेतदुपनीतं मे परमप्रीणनं सखे । तर्पयन्त्यङ्ग मां विश्वं एते पृथुकतण्डुलाः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: nanvetadupanītaṃ me paramaprīṇanaṃ sakhetarpayantyaṅga māṃ viśvaṃ ete pṛthukataṇḍulāḥ
translation: My dear friend! This present (parched rice-eatable) is exceedingly to my liking. These flattened parched rice will thoroughly satisfy me who am the Soul of the universe.”

devanagari: इति मुष्टिं सकृज्जग्ध्वा द्वितीयां जग्धुमाददे । तावच्छ्रीर्जगृहे हस्तं तत्परा परमेष्ठिनः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: iti muṣṭiṃ sakṛjjagdhvā dvitīyāṃ jagdhumādadetāvacchrīrjagṛhe hastaṃ tatparā parameṣṭhinaḥ
translation: Saying thus, he ate handful of those rice and took another handful for eating when his consort Śrī (i.e. Rukmiṇī who was so devoted to him caught hold of the hand of the Supreme[1] Lord, (claiming that other handful of rice to herself).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā explains the significance of this prohibition to eat another handful as follows: one handful is enough to bestow on the poor Brāhmaṇa all the wealth and affluence. But by eating another handful, she will be at the service of the Brāhmaṇa (instead of Lord Kṛṣṇa)—a calamity she wanted to avoid.

devanagari: एतावतालं विश्वात्मन् सर्वसम्पत्समृद्धये । अस्मिन्लोके अथवामुष्मिन् पुंसस्त्वत्तोषकारणम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: etāvatālaṃ viśvātman sarvasampatsamṛddhayeasminloke athavāmuṣmin puṃsastvattoṣakāraṇam
translation: “O Soul of the Universe! This (handful of eaten rice) is more than sufficient in granting him all kinds of wealth and prosperity both here and hereafter. This much is quite enough for your satisfaction.”

devanagari: ब्राह्मणस्तां तु रजनीं उषित्वाच्युतमन्दिरे । भुक्त्वा पीत्वा सुखं मेने आत्मानं स्वर्गतं यथा ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: brāhmaṇastāṃ tu rajanīṃ uṣitvācyutamandirebhuktvā pītvā sukhaṃ mene ātmānaṃ svargataṃ yathā
translation: The Brāhmaṇa passed that night happily in the palace of Śrī Kṛṣṇa. He partook of the feast and drink and felt himself to be in the celestial world itself.

devanagari: श्वोभूते विश्वभावेन स्वसुखेनाभिवन्दितः । जगाम स्वालयं तात पथ्यनुव्रज्य नन्दितः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: śvobhūte viśvabhāvena svasukhenābhivanditaḥjagāma svālayaṃ tāta pathyanuvrajya nanditaḥ
translation: The next morning, he set out for his home. He was however, followed some distance by Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Creator of the Universe, the very embodiment of essential spiritual bliss who delighted him with his friendly talk.

devanagari: स चालब्ध्वा धनं कृष्णान् न तु याचितवान् स्वयम् । स्वगृहान् व्रीडितोऽगच्छन् महद्दर्शननिर्वृतः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa cālabdhvā dhanaṃ kṛṣṇān na tu yācitavān svayamsvagṛhān vrīḍito'gacchan mahaddarśananirvṛtaḥ
translation: But the Brāhmaṇa who was happy with seeing his great friend, returned home abashed on account of the meanness of his heart as he did not get any money from Kṛṣṇa nor did he of his own accord request him for it.

devanagari: अहो ब्रह्मण्यदेवस्य दृष्टा ब्रह्मण्यता मया । यद् दरिद्रतमो लक्ष्मीं आश्लिष्टो बिभ्रतोरसि ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: aho brahmaṇyadevasya dṛṣṭā brahmaṇyatā mayāyad daridratamo lakṣmīṃ āśliṣṭo bibhratorasi
translation: (On the way, he said to himself): “How wonderful! The Lord is known for his friendliness to Brāhmaṇas which has been personally experienced by me, as the bearer of the goddess Lakṣmī on his bosom held me in close embrace—me who am the wṛetchedmost.

devanagari: क्वाहं दरिद्रः पापीयान् क्व कृष्णः श्रीनिकेतनः । ब्रह्मबन्धुरिति स्माहं बाहुभ्यां परिरम्भितः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: kvāhaṃ daridraḥ pāpīyān kva kṛṣṇaḥ śrīniketanaḥbrahmabandhuriti smāhaṃ bāhubhyāṃ parirambhitaḥ
translation: What a world of difference there is between me, a poverty-stricken sinner and Lord Kṛṣṇa, the abode of Lakṣmī. But I have been embraced by him with both of his arms because of my birth as a Brāhmaṇa (so kind and friendly he is to Brāhmaṇas).

devanagari: निवासितः प्रियाजुष्टे पर्यङ्के भ्रातरो यथा । महिष्या वीजितः श्रान्तो बालव्यजनहस्तया ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: nivāsitaḥ priyājuṣṭe paryaṅke bhrātaro yathāmahiṣyā vījitaḥ śrānto bālavyajanahastayā
translation: Just like a brother, I was seated on the same sofa that was occupied by his beloved queen. Fatigued as I was, I was fanned by the Queen herself with a chowry in her hand.

devanagari: शुश्रूषया परमया पादसंवाहनादिभिः । पूजितो देवदेवेन विप्रदेवेन देववत् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: śuśrūṣayā paramayā pādasaṃvāhanādibhiḥpūjito devadevena vipradevena devavat
translation: I was adored like a god by Kṛṣṇa, the God of gods who treats Brāhmaṇas as his deities. Hs rendered me the highest service by personally shampooing my feet, applying sandalpastes, etc.

devanagari: स्वर्गापवर्गयोः पुंसां रसायां भुवि सम्पदाम् । सर्वासामपि सिद्धीनां मूलं तच्चरणार्चनम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: svargāpavargayoḥ puṃsāṃ rasāyāṃ bhuvi sampadāmsarvāsāmapi siddhīnāṃ mūlaṃ taccaraṇārcanam
translation: Worship of his feet is the primary cause for men to be blessed with happiness in heaven or the bliss in the Mokṣa and all the affluence available either in this world or the nether-world and of mastery over all miraculous powers (Siddhis).

devanagari: अधनोऽयं धनं प्राप्य माद्यात् उच्चैः न मां स्मरेत् । इति कारुणिको नूनं धनं मेऽभूरि नाददात् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: adhano'yaṃ dhanaṃ prāpya mādyāt uccaiḥ na māṃ smaretiti kāruṇiko nūnaṃ dhanaṃ me'bhūri nādadāt
translation: The Lord thought that if a penniless fellow (like me) should gain wealth, it would turn his head and would not remember me. It is with this consideration that the merciful Lord has not bestowed any wealth on me.”

devanagari: इति तच्चिन्तयन् अन्तः प्राप्तो नियगृहान्तिकम् । सूर्यानलेन्दुसङ्काशैः विमानैः सर्वतो वृतम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: iti taccintayan antaḥ prāpto niyagṛhāntikamsūryānalendusaṅkāśaiḥ vimānaiḥ sarvato vṛtam
translation: While he was musing thus, he arrived at the site of his home-stead. And Lo! It was surrounded on all sides by a palace several storeys high and resplendent like the sun, fire and the moon.

devanagari: विचित्रोपवनोद्यानैः कूजद्‍द्विजकुलाकुलैः । प्रोत्फुल्लकमुदाम्भोज कह्लारोत्पलवारिभिः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: vicitropavanodyānaiḥ kūjad‍dvijakulākulaiḥprotphullakamudāmbhoja kahlārotpalavāribhiḥ
translation: It was surrounded with wonderfully beautiful pleasure gardens and groves swarming with the melodiously warbling birds and with pools and tanks in which, various types of lilies, lotuses, night-lotuses were in full blossom.

devanagari: जुष्टं स्वलङ्कृतैः पुम्भिः स्त्रीभिश्च हरिणाक्षिभिः । किमिदं कस्य वा स्थानं कथं तदिदमित्यभूत् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: juṣṭaṃ svalaṅkṛtaiḥ pumbhiḥ strībhiśca hariṇākṣibhiḥkimidaṃ kasya vā sthānaṃ kathaṃ tadidamityabhūt
translation: It was manned by well-adorned male attendants and beautiful female attendants with gazelle-like eyes. The Brāhmaṇa could not recognise what it was, whose place it was and how was it that it was transformed so wonderfully.

devanagari: एवं मीमांसमानं तं नरा नार्योऽमरप्रभाः । प्रत्यगृह्णन् महाभागं गीतवाद्येन भूयसा ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ mīmāṃsamānaṃ taṃ narā nāryo'maraprabhāḥpratyagṛhṇan mahābhāgaṃ gītavādyena bhūyasā
translation: While the Brāhmaṇa was pondering over that metamorphosis (of his humble home-stead) god-like glamorous men and women advanced to welcome the highly fortunate Brāhmaṇa with excellent vocal and instrumental music.[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
VT in his com. on Verse 22-23 clarifies that when Śrīdāman stayed that night at Dvārakā and his wife in her humble cottage, this miraculous transformation of her cottage took place. When she got up and was astonished to see the change, the attendants explained how this metamorphosis was due to Lord Kṛṣṇa’s, Grace. So in order that her husband should not feel confused, she instructed the attendants to receive him, etc.

devanagari: पतिमागतमाकर्ण्य पत्‍न्युद्धर्षातिसम्भ्रमा । निश्चक्राम गृहात्तूर्णं रूपिणी श्रीरिवालयात् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: patimāgatamākarṇya pat‍nyuddharṣātisambhramāniścakrāma gṛhāttūrṇaṃ rūpiṇī śrīrivālayāt
translation: Hearing that her husband had returned, the Brāhmaṇa’s wife was transported with ecstatic delight. And in her joyous impatience, she hurriedly came out of the house (to receive him) like unto the goddess Lakṣmī incarnate coming out of her (heavenly) abode.

devanagari: पतिव्रता पतिं दृष्ट्वा प्रेमोत्कण्ठाश्रुलोचना । मीलिताक्ष्यनमद् बुद्ध्या मनसा परिषस्वजे ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: pativratā patiṃ dṛṣṭvā premotkaṇṭhāśrulocanāmīlitākṣyanamad buddhyā manasā pariṣasvaje
translation: At the sight of her husband, the chaste lady had her eyes full of tears of love and eagerness. (Out of shyness) she closed her eyes and mentally paid respects to him and embraced him in her mind.

devanagari: पत्‍नीं वीक्ष्य विस्फुरन्तीं देवीं वैमानिकीमिव । दासीनां निष्ककण्ठीनां मध्ये भान्तीं स विस्मितः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: pat‍nīṃ vīkṣya visphurantīṃ devīṃ vaimānikīmivadāsīnāṃ niṣkakaṇṭhīnāṃ madhye bhāntīṃ sa vismitaḥ
translation: He was astonished to see his wife gloriously glamorous like a celestial damsel and shining (in her deportment) in the midst of maid-servants adorned with necklaces of gold coins.

devanagari: प्रीतः स्वयं तया युक्तः प्रविष्टो निजमन्दिरम् । मणिस्तम्भशतोपेतं महेन्द्रभवनं यथा ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: prītaḥ svayaṃ tayā yuktaḥ praviṣṭo nijamandirammaṇistambhaśatopetaṃ mahendrabhavanaṃ yathā
translation: Being beside himself with joy, he entered in the company of his wife, his own mansion which was furnished with hundred(s) of columns of precious stones and which resembled the palace of the great Indra.

devanagari: पयःफेननिभाः शय्या दान्ता रुक्मपरिच्छदाः । पर्यङ्का हेमदण्डानि चामरव्यजनानि च ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: payaḥphenanibhāḥ śayyā dāntā rukmaparicchadāḥparyaṅkā hemadaṇḍāni cāmaravyajanāni ca
translation: His palace was furnished with beds white (and soft) like the foam of milk. The bedsteads were of ivory chased in gold and the fans and chowries had gold handles.

devanagari: आसनानि च हैमानि मृदूपस्तरणानि च । मुक्तादामविलम्बीनि वितानानि द्युमन्ति च ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: āsanāni ca haimāni mṛdūpastaraṇāni camuktādāmavilambīni vitānāni dyumanti ca
translation: The seats were of gold provided with soft cushions. And wreathes of pearls were hanging from brilliant canopies.

devanagari: स्वच्छस्फटिककुड्येषु महामारकतेषु च । रत्‍नदीपा भ्राजमानान् ललना रत्‍नसंयुताः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: svacchasphaṭikakuḍyeṣu mahāmārakateṣu carat‍nadīpā bhrājamānān lalanā rat‍nasaṃyutāḥ
translation: The palace walls were of pure crystals studded with big emeralds and in them he saw excellent dolls of precious stones holding refulgent jewel lamps in their hands (to illumine the room).

devanagari: विलोक्य ब्राह्मणस्तत्र समृद्धीः सर्वसम्पदाम् । तर्कयामास निर्व्यग्रः स्वसमृद्धिमहैतुकीम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: vilokya brāhmaṇastatra samṛddhīḥ sarvasampadāmtarkayāmāsa nirvyagraḥ svasamṛddhimahaitukīm
translation: Beholding such abundance of wealth, affluence and luxury, the Brāhmaṇa, with a dispassionate mind began to reflect upon that ‘cause-less’ un-sought-for windfall of wealth in his care.

devanagari: नूनं बतैतन्मम दुर्भगस्य । शश्वद् दरिद्रस्य समृद्धिहेतुः । महाविभूतेरवलोकतोऽन्यो । नैवोपपद्येत यदूत्तमस्य ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: nūnaṃ bataitanmama durbhagasyaśaśvad daridrasya samṛddhihetuḥmahāvibhūteravalokato'nyonaivopapadyeta yadūttamasya
translation: “I am indeed unfortunate (from my childhood) and am confirmed in eternal poverty. But the cause of this unexpected prosperity can be traced to nothing else but the gracious visit (I had the privilege to obtain) of Lord Kṛṣṇa, the leader of Yadus, the master of infinite wealth.

devanagari: नन्वब्रुवाणो दिशते समक्षं । याचिष्णवे भूर्यपि भूरिभोजः । पर्जन्यवत्तत् स्वयमीक्षमाणो । दाशार्हकाणामृषभः सखा मे ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: nanvabruvāṇo diśate samakṣaṃyāciṣṇave bhūryapi bhūribhojaḥparjanyavattat svayamīkṣamāṇodāśārhakāṇāmṛṣabhaḥ sakhā me
translation: My friend, the foremost of the Dāśārhas is infinitely liberal and is perfect in all blessings. Though he bestows abundantly upon his suppliants like the rain-god, he regards his gift as insignificant and nominal. Knowing the minds of his devotees he gives immense riches to them but does not promise them directly. (He is a silent but highly munificent donor).

devanagari: किञ्चित्करोत्युर्वपि यत् स्वदत्तं । सुहृत्कृतं फल्ग्वपि भूरिकारी । मयोपणीतं पृथुकैकमुष्टिं । प्रत्यग्रहीत् प्रीतियुतो महात्मा ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: kiñcitkarotyurvapi yat svadattaṃsuhṛtkṛtaṃ phalgvapi bhūrikārīmayopaṇītaṃ pṛthukaikamuṣṭiṃpratyagrahīt prītiyuto mahātmā
translation: Although what he gives is immensely abundant, he looks upon it as very little. And whatever insignificant is offered by his friends, he regards it as very great. That high-souled affectionate friend accepted with pleasure only a handful of the parched, flattened rice taken by me for him.

devanagari: तस्यैव मे सौहृदसख्यमैत्री । दास्यं पुनर्जन्मनि जन्मनि स्यात् । महानुभावेन गुणालयेन । विषज्जतः तत्पुरुषप्रसङ्गः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyaiva me sauhṛdasakhyamaitrīdāsyaṃ punarjanmani janmani syātmahānubhāvena guṇālayenaviṣajjataḥ tatpuruṣaprasaṅgaḥ
translation: May I, in every future birth, be blessed with his good-will, friendship, love and service. May I have the fortune of associating myself with his devotees and may I cherish (ever-increasing) devotion to that abode of excellences and glorious powers.

devanagari: भक्ताय चित्रा भगवान् हि सम्पदो । राज्यं विभूतीर्न समर्थयत्यजः । अदीर्घबोधाय विचक्षणः स्वयं । पश्यन् निपातं धनिनां मदोद्भवम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: bhaktāya citrā bhagavān hi sampadorājyaṃ vibhūtīrna samarthayatyajaḥadīrghabodhāya vicakṣaṇaḥ svayaṃpaśyan nipātaṃ dhanināṃ madodbhavam
translation: For the Eternal Omniscient glorious Lord himself notices the downfall of the rich caused by the pride of their wealth, and does not wish to confer wonderful affluence, king- ship and other spiritual powers or authorities on his devotee (even though he may solicit it) but grants them to the shortsighted ones.

devanagari: इत्थं व्यवसितो बुद्ध्या भक्तोऽतीव जनार्दने । विषयान्जायया त्यक्ष्यन् बुभुजे नातिलम्पटः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ vyavasito buddhyā bhakto'tīva janārdaneviṣayānjāyayā tyakṣyan bubhuje nātilampaṭaḥ
translation: Having come to this conclusion by intelligent thinking, the Brāhmaṇa who was devoutly and excessively attached to Kṛṣṇa, did not become much attached to those pleasures, but with the intention of renouncing them, he enjoyed those pleasures with his wife, but with little attachment to them.

devanagari: तस्य वै देवदेवस्य हरेर्यज्ञपतेः प्रभोः । ब्राह्मणाः प्रभवो दैवं न तेभ्यो विद्यते परम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya vai devadevasya hareryajñapateḥ prabhoḥbrāhmaṇāḥ prabhavo daivaṃ na tebhyo vidyate param
translation: To the Almighty Lord Hari, the God of gods, the Lord and Protector of Sacrifices, Brāhmaṇas are his deities. To him, nothing is higher then Brāhmaṇas.

devanagari: एवं स विप्रो भगवत्सुहृत्तदा । दृष्ट्वा स्वभृत्यैरजितं पराजितम् । तद्ध्यानवेगोद्ग्रथितात्मबन्धनः । तद्धाम लेभेऽचिरतः सतां गतिम् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ sa vipro bhagavatsuhṛttadādṛṣṭvā svabhṛtyairajitaṃ parājitamtaddhyānavegodgrathitātmabandhanaḥtaddhāma lebhe'cirataḥ satāṃ gatim
translation: In this way, the Brāhmaṇa friend of the Lord realized that the Unvanquished Lord Kṛṣṇa is easily agreeable to the will of his devotees. His personal bonds (of ignorance or attachment) were cut asunder by the force of his intensive meditation. And in a short while, he attained to the Lord’s region (Vaikuṇṭha), the highest goal of the righteous people.

devanagari: एतद् ब्रह्मण्यदेवस्य श्रुत्वा ब्रह्मण्यतां नरः । लब्धभावो भगवति कर्मबन्धाद् विमुच्यते ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: etad brahmaṇyadevasya śrutvā brahmaṇyatāṃ naraḥlabdhabhāvo bhagavati karmabandhād vimucyate
translation:
A person who listens to this account of the grace showered on a Brāhmaṇa by the God who is friendly to Brāhmaṇas attains devotion unto the Lord and becomes free from the bonds of Karmas.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । पृथुकोपाख्यानं नाम एकशीतितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ८१ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃsaṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhepṛthukopākhyānaṃ nāma ekaśītitamo'dhyāyaḥ
